heading: Border security metrics
label: 223 Border security metrics
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2017, and not as part of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 which comprises this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Homeland Security of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Consequence Delivery System
children.label: 2 Consequence Delivery System
children.text: The term “Consequence Delivery System” means the series of consequences applied by U.S. Border Patrol in collaboration with other Federal agencies to persons unlawfully entering the United States, in order to prevent unlawful border crossing recidivism.

children.label: A
children.text: is directly or indirectly observed making an unlawful entry into the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: is not apprehended; and

children.label: C
children.text: is not a turn back.

children.label: A
children.text: interdicted in the waters over which the United States has jurisdiction;

children.label: B
children.text: identified at sea either directly or indirectly, but not interdicted;

children.label: C
children.text: if not described in subparagraph (A) or (B), who were otherwise reported, with a significant degree of certainty, as having entered, or attempted to enter, the United States through the maritime border.

children.label: A
children.text: Possession of illicit drugs.

children.label: B
children.text: Smuggling of prohibited products.

children.label: C
children.text: Human smuggling.

children.label: D
children.text: Possession of illegal weapons.

children.label: E
children.text: Use of fraudulent documents.

children.label: F
children.text: Any other offense that is serious enough to result in an arrest.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 6 Secretary
children.text: The term “the Secretary” means the Secretary of Homeland Security.

children.label: A
children.text: Threats and trends concerning illicit trafficking and unlawful crossings.

children.label: B
children.text: The ability to forecast future shifts in such threats and trends.

children.label: C
children.text: The ability to evaluate such threats and trends at a level sufficient to create actionable plans.

children.label: D
children.text: The operational capability to conduct persistent and integrated surveillance of the international borders of the United States.

children.heading: Transit zone
children.label: 8 Transit zone
children.text: The term “transit zone” means the sea corridors of the western Atlantic Ocean, the Gulf of Mexico, the Caribbean Sea, and the eastern Pacific Ocean through which undocumented migrants and illicit drugs transit, either directly or indirectly, to the United States.

children.heading: Turn back
children.label: 9 Turn back
children.text: The term “turn back” means an unlawful border crosser who, after making an unlawful entry into the United States, responds to United States enforcement efforts by returning promptly to the country from which such crosser entered.

children.heading: Unlawful border crossing effectiveness rate
children.label: 10 Unlawful border crossing effectiveness rate
children.text: The term “unlawful border crossing effectiveness rate” means the percentage that results from dividing the number of apprehensions and turn backs by the sum of the number of apprehensions, estimated undetected unlawful entries, turn backs, and got aways.

children.heading: Unlawful entry
children.label: 11 Unlawful entry
children.text: The term “unlawful entry” means an unlawful border crosser who enters the United States and is not apprehended by a border security component of the Department of Homeland Security.

children.heading: Metrics for securing the border between ports of entry
children.label: b Metrics for securing the border between ports of entry
i
children.text: The rate of apprehension of attempted unlawful border crossers.

children.label: ii
children.text: The number of detected unlawful entries.

children.label: iii
children.text: The number of estimated undetected unlawful entries.

children.label: iv
children.text: Turn backs.

children.label: v
children.text: Got aways.

children.label: B
children.text: A measurement of situational awareness achieved in each U.S. Border Patrol sector.

children.label: C
children.text: An unlawful border crossing effectiveness rate in each U.S. Border Patrol sector.

children.label: D
children.text: A probability of detection rate, which compares the estimated total unlawful border crossing attempts not detected by U.S. Border Patrol to the unlawful border crossing effectiveness rate under subparagraph (C), as informed by subparagraph (A).

children.label: E
children.text: The number of apprehensions in each U.S. Border Patrol sector.

children.label: F
children.text: The number of apprehensions of unaccompanied alien children, and the nationality of such children, in each U.S. Border Patrol sector.

children.label: G
children.text: The number of apprehensions of family units, and the nationality of such family units, in each U.S. Border Patrol sector.

children.label: H
children.text: An illicit drugs seizure rate for drugs seized by U.S. Border Patrol between ports of entry, which compares the ratio of the amount and type of illicit drugs seized between ports of entry in any fiscal year to the average of the amount and type of illicit drugs seized between ports of entry in the immediately preceding five fiscal years.

children.label: I
children.text: Estimates of the impact of the Consequence Delivery System on the rate of recidivism of unlawful border crossers over multiple fiscal years.

children.label: i
children.text: Voluntary return.

children.label: ii
children.text: Warrant of arrest or notice to appear.

children.label: iii
children.text: Expedited removal.

children.label: iv
children.text: Reinstatement of removal.

children.label: v
children.text: Alien transfer exit program.

children.label: vi
children.text: Criminal consequence program.

children.label: vii
children.text: Standard prosecution.

children.label: viii
children.text: Operation Against Smugglers Initiative on Safety and Security.

children.label: A
children.text: consult with the heads of the appropriate components of the Department of Homeland Security; and

children.label: B
children.text: where appropriate, with the heads of other agencies, including the Office of Refugee Resettlement of the Department of Health and Human Services and the Executive Office for Immigration Review of the Department of Justice.

children.heading: Manner of collection
children.label: 3 Manner of collection
children.text: The data collected to inform the metrics developed in accordance with paragraph (1) shall be collected and reported in a consistent and standardized manner across all U.S. Border Patrol sectors, informed by situational awareness.

children.heading: Metrics for securing the border at ports of entry
children.label: c Metrics for securing the border at ports of entry
i
children.text: Total inadmissible travelers who attempt to, or successfully, enter the United States at a port of entry.

children.label: ii
children.text: The rate of refusals and interdictions for travelers who attempt to, or successfully, enter the United States at a port of entry.

children.label: iii
children.text: The number of unlawful entries at a port of entry.

children.label: B
children.text: The amount and type of illicit drugs seized by the Office of Field Operations of U.S. Customs and Border Protection at ports of entry during the previous fiscal year.

children.label: C
children.text: An illicit drugs seizure rate for drugs seized by the Office of Field Operations, which compares the ratio of the amount and type of illicit drugs seized by the Office of Field Operations in any fiscal year to the average of the amount and type of illicit drugs seized by the Office of Field Operations in the immediately preceding five fiscal years.

children.label: D
children.text: The number of infractions related to travelers and cargo committed by major violators who are interdicted by the Office of Field Operations at ports of entry, and the estimated number of such infractions committed by major violators who are not so interdicted.

children.label: E
children.text: In consultation with the heads of the Office of National Drug Control Policy and the United States Southern Command, a cocaine seizure effectiveness rate, which is the percentage resulting from dividing the amount of cocaine seized by the Office of Field Operations by the total estimated cocaine flow rate at ports of entry along the United States land border with Mexico and Canada.

children.label: i
children.text: A wait time ratio that compares the average wait times to total commercial and private vehicular traffic volumes at each land port of entry.

children.label: ii
children.text: An infrastructure capacity utilization rate that measures traffic volume against the physical and staffing capacity at each land port of entry.

children.label: iii
children.text: A secondary examination rate that measures the frequency of secondary examinations at each land port of entry.

children.label: iv
children.text: An enforcement rate that measures the effectiveness of such secondary examinations at detecting major violators.

children.label: i
children.text: The number of all cargo containers that are considered potentially “high-risk”, as determined by the Executive Assistant Commissioner of the Office of Field Operations.

children.label: ii
children.text: A comparison of the number of potentially high-risk cargo containers scanned by the Office of Field Operations at each sea port of entry during a fiscal year to the total number of high-risk cargo containers entering the United States at each such sea port of entry during the previous fiscal year.

children.label: iii
children.text: The number of potentially high-risk cargo containers scanned upon arrival at a United States sea port of entry.

children.label: iv
children.text: The number of potentially high-risk cargo containers scanned before arrival at a United States sea port of entry.

children.label: A
children.text: consult with the heads of the appropriate components of the Department of Homeland Security; and

children.label: B
children.text: where appropriate, work with heads of other appropriate agencies, including the Office of Refugee Resettlement of the Department of Health and Human Services and the Executive Office for Immigration Review of the Department of Justice.

children.heading: Manner of collection
children.label: 3 Manner of collection
children.text: The data collected to inform the metrics developed in accordance with paragraph (1) shall be collected and reported in a consistent and standardized manner across all United States ports of entry, informed by situational awareness.

children.heading: Metrics for securing the maritime border
children.label: d Metrics for securing the maritime border
A
children.text: Situational awareness achieved in the maritime environment.

children.label: B
children.text: A known maritime migrant flow rate.

children.label: C
children.text: An illicit drugs removal rate for drugs removed inside and outside of a transit zone, which compares the amount and type of illicit drugs removed, including drugs abandoned at sea, by the maritime security components of the Department of Homeland Security in any fiscal year to the average of the amount and type of illicit drugs removed by such maritime components for the immediately preceding five fiscal years.

children.label: D
children.text: In consultation with the heads of the Office of National Drug Control Policy and the United States Southern Command, a cocaine removal effectiveness rate for cocaine removed inside a transit zone and outside a transit zone, which compares the amount of cocaine removed by the maritime security components of the Department of Homeland Security by the total documented cocaine flow rate, as contained in Federal drug databases.

children.label: E
children.text: A response rate, which compares the ability of the maritime security components of the Department of Homeland Security to respond to and resolve known maritime threats, whether inside or outside a transit zone, by placing assets on-scene, to the total number of events with respect to which the Department has known threat information.

children.label: F
children.text: An intergovernmental response rate, which compares the ability of the maritime security components of the Department of Homeland Security or other United States Government entities to respond to and resolve actionable maritime threats, whether inside or outside a transit zone, with the number of such threats detected.

children.label: A
children.text: consult with the heads of the appropriate components of the Department of Homeland Security; and

children.label: B
children.text: where appropriate, work with the heads of other agencies, including the Drug Enforcement Agency, the Department of Defense, and the Department of Justice.

children.heading: Manner of collection
children.label: 3 Manner of collection
children.text: The data used by the Secretary shall be collected and reported in a consistent and standardized manner by the maritime security components of the Department of Homeland Security, informed by situational awareness.

children.heading: Air and Marine security metrics in the land domain
children.label: e Air and Marine security metrics in the land domain
A
children.text: A flight hour effectiveness rate, which compares Air and Marine Operations flight hours requirements to the number of flight hours flown by Air and Marine Operations.

children.label: B
children.text: A funded flight hour effectiveness rate, which compares the number of funded flight hours appropriated to Air and Marine Operations to the number of actual flight hours flown by Air and Marine Operations.

children.label: C
children.text: A readiness rate, which compares the number of aviation missions flown by Air and Marine Operations to the number of aviation missions cancelled by Air and Marine Operations due to maintenance, operations, or other causes.

children.label: D
children.text: The number of missions cancelled by Air and Marine Operations due to weather compared to the total planned missions.

children.label: E
children.text: The number of individuals detected by Air and Marine Operations through the use of unmanned aerial systems and manned aircraft.

children.label: F
children.text: The number of apprehensions assisted by Air and Marine Operations through the use of unmanned aerial systems and manned aircraft.

children.label: G
children.text: The number and quantity of illicit drug seizures assisted by Air and Marine Operations through the use of unmanned aerial systems and manned aircraft.

children.label: H
children.text: The number of times that actionable intelligence related to border security was obtained through the use of unmanned aerial systems and manned aircraft.

children.label: A
children.text: consult with the heads of the appropriate components of the Department of Homeland Security; and

children.label: B
children.text: as appropriate, work with the heads of other departments and agencies, including the Department of Justice.

children.heading: Manner of collection
children.label: 3 Manner of collection
children.text: The data collected to inform the metrics developed in accordance with paragraph (1) shall be collected and reported in a consistent and standardized manner by Air and Marine Operations, informed by situational awareness.

in accordance with applicable privacy laws, make data related to apprehensions, inadmissible aliens, drug seizures, and other enforcement actions available to the public, law enforcement communities, and academic research communities; and
provide the Office of Immigration Statistics of the Department of Homeland Security with unfettered access to the data referred to in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Evaluation by the Government Accountability Office and the Secretary
children.label: g Evaluation by the Government Accountability Office and the Secretary

children.heading: Mandatory disclosures
children.label: A Mandatory disclosures
children.text: The Secretary shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees and the Comptroller General of the United States an annual report containing the metrics required under this section and the data and methodology used to develop such metrics.

children.label: i
children.text: the Center for Borders, Trade, and Immigration Research of the Centers of Excellence network of the Department of Homeland Security;

children.label: ii
children.text: the head of a national laboratory within the Department of Homeland Security laboratory network with prior expertise in border security; and

children.label: iii
children.text: a Federally Funded Research and Development Center.

children.label: A
children.text: analyzes the suitability and statistical validity of the data and methodology contained in each such report; and

children.label: i
children.text: the feasibility of other suitable metrics that may be used to measure the effectiveness of border security; and

children.label: ii
children.text: improvements that need to be made to the metrics being used to measure the effectiveness of border security.

children.label: A
children.text: provides trends for each metric under this section for the last ten fiscal years, to the greatest extent possible;

children.label: B
children.text: provides selected analysis into related aspects of illegal flow rates, including undocumented migrant flows and stock estimation techniques;

children.label: C
children.text: provides selected analysis into related aspects of legal flow rates; and

children.label: D
children.text: includes any other information that the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: After submitting the tenth report to the Comptroller General under paragraph (1), the Secretary may reevaluate and update any of the metrics developed in accordance with this section to ensure that such metrics are suitable to measure the effectiveness of border security.

children.heading: Congressional notification
children.label: B Congressional notification
children.text: Not later than 30 days before updating the metrics pursuant to subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall notify the appropriate congressional committees of such updates.
